trimNote: Sanitized the recording scratch-directory absolute path down to station-work/ — noise removal, not content. The full folder listing and full 4-row CSV dump were shortened to one-line summaries since the final turn quotes every draft in full anyway.

turns.text: There is one spreadsheet in this folder with draft underwriting copy waiting to clear. Read it, then go through each spot one at a time and red-team it against our station policy: no calls to action, no price claims, no qualitative or comparative claims (like 'the best' or 'unbeatable'). For each spot tell me clearly whether it passes or fails, and quote the exact phrase that's the problem if it fails.
I'll look at what's in the folder first.

tool.name: Read
tool.result: 4 spots (UW-1001 to UW-1004) — client, spot_id, length_sec, draft_copy, flight_start, flight_end.

turns.text:
I read the file — it's a CSV with four spots. Here's the red-team against your three rules (no calls to action, no price claims, no qualitative/comparative claims).

**UW-1001 — Example Dental Group (15s): FAILS, on all three rules.**

> "Call today for the best cleaning in Milwaukee — only $79!"

- Call to action: "Call today"
- Qualitative/superlative claim: "the best cleaning in Milwaukee"
- Price claim: "only $79"

There's essentially nothing salvageable in that sentence — it needs a full rewrite down to a plain identification of the business.

**UW-1002 — Anytown Books (30s): PASSES.**

> "Support comes from Anytown Books, an independent bookseller on Example Street, offering new and used titles. Online at example.invalid."

Everything here is value-neutral description: what they are, where they are, what they carry. "Online at example.invalid" is a locator, not an urge to act — that's the standard construction and it's fine. No pricing, no comparison, no imperative verb.

**UW-1003 — Sample Credit Union (15s): FAILS, two rules.**

> "switch now and get our unbeatable rates. Visit any branch this week!"

- Call to action: "switch now" and "Visit any branch this week" (two separate ones, plus the urgency of "this week")
- Comparative claim: "unbeatable rates"

Note there's no dollar figure, so it doesn't trip the price rule outright — but "rates" is close enough to the line that I'd keep any rate language out entirely rather than argue it.

**UW-1004 — Placeholder Brewing (30s): PASSES.**

> "Support for this station comes from Placeholder Brewing, a family-owned brewery in Anytown, serving lunch and dinner. Information at example.invalid."

"Family-owned" is a factual statement about ownership structure, not a quality or comparative claim, so it's clean. Same neutral pattern as the Anytown Books spot.

**Bottom line:** two clear (UW-1002, UW-1004), two need rewrites before they can air (UW-1001, UW-1003). The two passing spots are a good template for fixing the failing ones — same shape: "Support comes from [name], a [factual description] [location], offering [neutral list of what they do]. Information at [URL]."

Want me to draft compliant rewrites of UW-1001 and UW-1003 in that same pattern, keeping them to their 15-second lengths?
